headline: CRUDE EXPLODES 5% ON IRAN CLOSURE THREAT
subheadline: Energy leads commodities higher as Tehran cuts diplomatic ties, threatens Hormuz blockade.
lead: Crude hit $94.30, up 5.1% and the biggest single-day move since March, after Iran cut diplomatic channels with Washington and threatened full Strait of Hormuz closure. The geopolitical premium that unwound through Memorial Day is back with interest, energy dragging the entire commodity complex higher except grains, which shed another 1% as the funds liquidate into June. The question this week: does the Iran flare sustain or fade like the last three iterations since 2024.
the_takeaway: Iran premium back, but grain liquidation continues through planting's end.
teaser: Energy explodes on Iran threats while grain funds keep booking Memorial profits.

one_number.unit: crude's intraday spike before settling at 5.1%
one_number.context: The overnight move hit 7% before profit-taking pulled it back, still the biggest energy move since March's Iran escalation. Shows the market's hair-trigger on Hormuz threats. Last time crude spiked this hard on geopolitical news, it gave back half the gain within a week.

yesterdays_call.summary: Geopolitical premium unwinding faster than it built, energy complex in full retreat.
yesterdays_call.note: Iran flipped the script overnight with diplomatic cutoff and Hormuz threats.

sections.title: Energy Explodes on Iran Escalation
sections.body: Crude ran to $94.30, up 5.1%, after Iran's state media reported Tehran is cutting diplomatic channels with Washington and moving to **fully block the Strait of Hormuz**. The overnight spike hit 7% before settling, still the biggest energy move since March. Natural gas fell 5.3% on the same news, funds rotating out of domestic supply stories into crude's geopolitical premium. Soybean oil caught the energy bid, up 2.5% as biodiesel math improves with higher petroleum prices.
sections.catalyst: Iran cuts U.S. diplomatic ties, threatens complete Hormuz closure
sections.bottom_line: Geopolitical premium back after three weeks of unwinding.

sections.title: Grains Extend Memorial Liquidation
sections.body: Corn dropped another 6 cents to $4.42, down 1.3% and the fourth straight session of fund selling into June. December corn shed 5¼ cents to $4.71, the carry still working as managed money books Memorial profits ahead of summer weather season. Soybeans eased 6 cents to $11.83, meal down 1.6% as crush margins compress on higher oil costs. **The grain liquidation is calendar-driven**, not fundamental, funds cleaning books before planting finishes and weather takes over.
sections.catalyst: Memorial Day fund liquidation extending into June planting finish
sections.bottom_line: Fund selling continues, calendar-driven through planting's end.

sections.title: Livestock Diverges Higher
sections.body: Live cattle gained 75 cents to $239.75, up 0.3%, feeders rose $2.48 to $350.98, up 0.7%, both breaking from the grain weakness. Lean hogs added 60 cents to $100.10, up 0.6%. **Class III milk exploded 3.7%** to $16.83, the biggest dairy move in a month as summer heat season begins and feed costs ease with grain liquidation. The livestock complex is pricing its own fundamentals, not following grain's Memorial fade.
sections.catalyst: Summer heat season begins, feed costs ease with grain liquidation
sections.bottom_line: Livestock pricing its own story, not following grain liquidation.

outside_the_pit.title: New World Screwworm Found 31 Miles from U.S. Border
outside_the_pit.body: The parasitic fly that eats warm-blooded animals alive showed up in Mexico, close enough to threaten U.S. cattle operations. Could cost millions in economic damage if it crosses, beef producers on high alert.

outside_the_pit.title: FTC Confirms Probe of Rising Fertilizer Prices
outside_the_pit.body: Federal Trade Commission launching antitrust investigation into fertilizer costs, looking at concentration in the industry. Input costs stayed elevated even as commodity prices pulled back from 2024 highs.

outside_the_pit.title: Russia Bans Jet Fuel Exports Through November
outside_the_pit.body: Moscow restricting aviation fuel exports as Ukrainian drone strikes hit refining capacity. Another supply disruption in global energy markets, supporting the Iran premium in crude.

spread_to_watch.label: December corn / July corn carry
spread_to_watch.commentary: The carry trade is Memorial liquidation's tell. Funds selling front months into storage, pushing December premium wider as they book profits before weather season. When the carry stops working, the liquidation is done.

basis.headline: Eastern Belt corn basis steady as ethanol grind recovers
basis.body: **Processor demand firming** east of the Mississippi as ethanol plants finish maintenance, basis holding despite futures weakness. Western Belt staying soft, consistent with the seasonal as river systems open for summer barge traffic. The local bid is pricing its own fundamentals while futures follow fund flow.

weekly_thread.question: Will the grain liquidation finish before weather takes control of the story?
weekly_thread.status_text: Fourth straight session of fund selling suggests Memorial profits still getting booked. The carry trade is working, December corn premium widening as front months get dumped. Weather season starts when the selling stops.

the_more_you_know.title: The carry trade is the planting calendar's tell
the_more_you_know.body: December corn traded 29 cents over July today, the widest carry since April. That spread tells you more about fund positioning than the headline price: when managed money liquidates front months into storage, the calendar spread widens. It's the most reliable signal that Memorial profits are still getting booked, not that fundamentals changed. When the carry stops working, the liquidation is done and weather takes control of the story.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress: corn above 85% planted removes weather premium, below 80% adds it back
Iran diplomatic response to Hormuz closure threats
Weekly export sales: soy under 400K MT keeps liquidation in charge

daily_quote.text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.

source_summary: CBOT, CME, NYMEX closes; Iran state media; USDA reports; trade flow data
